Title: The apocarotenoid metabolite zaxinone regulates growth and strigolactone biosynthesis in rice

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase (CCD) family; sixth CCD subfamily (ZAS)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream apocarotenoid cleavage step producing zaxinone from zeaxanthinal precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 3-OH-apocarotenals (zeaxanthinals) at the C13,C14 double bond to form zaxinone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and shoots, with strongest demonstrated metabolic effect in roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Positive regulator of rice growth and development; suppresses strigolactone biosynthesis and release; influences mycorrhization and Striga interactions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes zaxinone accumulation; loss of function lowers root zaxinone, retards growth, elevates strigolactone levels and release
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for zaxinone formation in rice roots; not sole source of zaxinone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with recombinant protein; HPLC/LC-MS and UHPLC-MS/MS product identification; Tos17 loss-of-function mutant analysis; in planta metabolite quantification; chemical rescue with exogenous zaxinone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript induced in roots under phosphate deficiency; increased during early and late stages of mycorrhization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed and phosphate-deficiency/mycorrhiza responsive in rice
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate deficiency; arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization; exogenous zaxinone treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenetic/genome survey of CCD orthologues across 69 land plant genomes; no accession explicitly provided for omics datasets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC_Os09g15240 encodes a previously overlooked CCD enzyme that produces zaxinone from hydroxylated apocarotenoids in vitro. 2. zas loss-of-function mutants contain less zaxinone in roots and accumulate more zeaxanthinal precursors, demonstrating an in planta role in zaxinone biosynthesis. 3. ZAS is required for normal rice growth and indirectly restrains strigolactone biosynthesis and release.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance rice growth and reduce Striga infestation by increasing zaxinone levels or ZAS activity; could be deployed in metabolic engineering or chemical biology strategies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of ZAS causes dwarfing, reduced root system, fewer tillers, panicles and seeds, and reduced mycorrhizal colonization; overengineering effects on SL-dependent symbiosis may require balancing
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice contains paralogues ZAS-L1, ZAS-L2 and ZAS-L3, and residual zaxinone in zas suggests additional ZAS-independent biosynthetic routes; Arabidopsis lacks ZAS orthologues but still contains zaxinone, indicating alternative formation routes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes all-trans-β-carotene to 9-cis-β-carotene for entry into strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture, mycorrhization and parasitic weed germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript level is elevated in zas under phosphate starvation and reduced by zaxinone treatment, consistent with lowered SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript analysis under phosphate starvation and zaxinone treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcript measured under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D27 transcript abundance increases in zas roots under low phosphate. 2. Zaxinone treatment suppresses D27 transcript levels under phosphate starvation. 3. D27 is included as part of the SL biosynthetic module negatively regulated by zaxinone.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/supporting pathway gene rather than the primary experimental focus

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-β-carotene to form 9-cis-β-apo-10'-carotenal in the SL pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture, mycorrhization and parasitic weed germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript level is elevated in zas under phosphate starvation and reduced by zaxinone treatment, consistent with lowered SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript analysis under phosphate starvation and zaxinone treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcript measured under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD7/D17 transcript abundance is higher in zas roots under low phosphate. 2. Zaxinone treatment suppresses CCD7/D17 transcript levels. 3. CCD7 is part of the zaxinone-responsive SL biosynthetic gene set.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/supporting pathway gene rather than the primary experimental focus

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 9-cis-β-apo-10'-carotenal into carlactone, the central intermediate of strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture, mycorrhization and parasitic weed germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript level is elevated in zas under phosphate starvation and reduced by zaxinone treatment, consistent with lowered SL biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript analysis under phosphate starvation and zaxinone treatment; mutant response to zaxinone in growth assays referenced
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcript measured under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD8/D10 transcript abundance is higher in zas roots under low phosphate. 2. Zaxinone treatment suppresses CCD8/D10 transcript levels. 3. Functional SL biosynthesis including CCD8 appears required for zaxinone-promoted root growth.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/supporting pathway gene rather than the primary experimental focus

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture, mycorrhization and parasitic weed germination
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript level is elevated in zas under phosphate starvation and reduced by zaxinone treatment, consistent with lowered 4-deoxyorobanchol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR transcript analysis under phosphate starvation and zaxinone treatment; LC-MS/MS quantification of 4-deoxyorobanchol as pathway output
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcript measured under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CO transcript abundance is higher in zas roots under low phosphate. 2. Zaxinone treatment suppresses CO transcript levels. 3. Reduced CO expression parallels reduced 4-deoxyorobanchol accumulation and release.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/supporting pathway gene rather than the primary experimental focus

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein involved in strigolactone signaling/feedback
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SL biosynthesis gene module including D27; CCD7/D17; CCD8/D10; CO
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for rac-GR24-mediated repression of SL biosynthetic transcripts, but not for zaxinone-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in negative feedback regulation of SL biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely SCF-type F-box complex, but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant architecture, mycorrhization and parasitic weed germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not required for zaxinone-induced reduction of SL levels and SL biosynthesis transcripts
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Serves as a comparator showing zaxinone acts independently of canonical D3-mediated SL feedback
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: d3 mutant treatment with zaxinone and rac-GR24; LC-MS/MS SL quantification; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; zaxinone treatment; rac-GR24 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Zaxinone still represses SL accumulation and biosynthetic transcripts in d3 mutant seedlings. 2. In contrast, rac-GR24 requires D3 to repress SL biosynthesis transcripts. 3. Therefore, D3 is dispensable for zaxinone signal transduction affecting SL biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Helps define signaling route for zaxinone-based agronomic application; indicates zaxinone can act independently of D3-mediated SL feedback
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulatory comparator rather than primary nutrient-metabolism discovery gene
